<skill name="mr-message" location="/home/jbarbosa/.pi/agent/skills/mr-message/SKILL.md">
References are relative to /home/jbarbosa/.pi/agent/skills/mr-message.

# MR Message

Generates a well-structured Merge Request message by analyzing the diff between the current branch and a target branch provided as an argument.

## Argument

`$1` — the target branch to diff against (e.g. `main`, `develop`). If not provided, default to `main`.

## Workflow

Follow these steps in order:

### 1. Gather context

Run the following commands in parallel:

```bash
# Commit log between target and current branch
git log $1..HEAD --oneline

# Full diff between target branch and current branch
git diff $1...HEAD

# Current branch name
git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD
```

### 2. Analyze the diff

From the diff and commit log, identify:
- **What changed**: files touched, modules affected, added/removed/modified behaviour
- **Why it changed**: infer intent from commit messages, variable names, and surrounding code
- **Type of change**: new feature, bug fix, refactor, docs, chore, config, test, etc.

### 3. Write the MR message

Produce a message in this structure:

```
## Summary

<1-3 bullet points describing what this MR does and why>

## Changes

<Bulleted list of notable changes, grouped logically if needed>
```

**Title** (one line, under 72 characters):
- Prefix with type: `feat:`, `fix:`, `refactor:`, `chore:`, etc.
- Imperative mood: "Add X", "Fix Y", "Remove Z"

## Rules

- Focus on the **why**, not just the what
- Be concise — avoid padding or filler sentences
- Group related changes together
- If the diff is large, summarize by area/module rather than listing every file
- Do not invent behaviour not present in the diff
- Output the title first, then the body in a single fenced block so it is easy to copy
</skill>

main

content.text:
8e37a20 add aya-react
090d3e3 add npm private registry
6330077 fix typo
a1c95b6 remove authentication leftovers
034d9bd remove MSAL

refactor: migrar autenticação de MSAL para Aya/Keycloak

```md
## Summary

- Troca a camada de autenticação antiga por `@ltplabs/aya-react`, alinhando o app ao fluxo com Keycloak.
- Remove rotas e telas de login/autorizaçao que deixaram de ser necessárias após a simplificação do acesso.
- Atualiza a dependência privada e limpa sobras de configuração do MSAL.

## Changes

- Substitui `MsalProvider`/`AuthContext`/configuração MSAL por `AuthProvider` da Aya, usando `VITE_KEYCLOAK_URL`, `VITE_REALM` e `VITE_OIDC_CLIENT_ID`.
- Remove as rotas `/login` e `/unauthorized`, além do wrapper de proteção; a navegação principal passa a renderizar diretamente.
- Elimina o botão de logout da sidebar junto com a lógica de autenticação local associada.
- Remove arquivos e código mortos do fluxo antigo de autenticação (`authConfig`, `msal`, `LoginPage`, `UnauthorizedPage`).
- Adiciona configuração do registry privado em `.npmrc` e inclui `@ltplabs/aya-react` em `package.json`/`package-lock.json`.
- Ajusta pequenos pontos colaterais, como o import do `flowchart.png` e a simplificação do bootstrap em `main.tsx`.
```

traduz para pt-pt

Add to your project's `.npmrc`:

```ini
@ltplabs:registry=https://git.ltplabs.net/api/v4/projects/640/packages/npm/
//git.ltplabs.net/api/v4/projects/640/packages/npm/:_authToken=<your-token>
```

>Tip: Instead of pasting the token in the file you can add GLAB_TOKEN as your env var in your shell
``` bash
echo "export GLAB_TOKEN=<token>" >> ~/.bashrc
```
and in your `.npmrc` instead of `<your-token>` write `${GLAB_TOKEN}`

Then:

```bash
npm install @ltplabs/aya-react
```

Get a token from GitLab → User → Preferences → Access Tokens (scope: `read_api`).

## Environment Variables

Create a `.env` file in your project root (copy from `.env.example`):

```ini
VITE_KEYCLOAK_URL=https://auth.example.com/
VITE_REALM=my-realm
VITE_OIDC_CLIENT_ID=my-client
```

Adicione ao `.npmrc` do seu projecto:

```ini
@ltplabs:registry=https://git.ltplabs.net/api/v4/projects/640/packages/npm/
//git.ltplabs.net/api/v4/projects/640/packages/npm/:_authToken=<o-seu-token>
```

> Sugestão: em vez de colar o token no ficheiro, pode adicionar `GLAB_TOKEN` como variável de ambiente na sua shell
```bash
echo "export GLAB_TOKEN=<token>" >> ~/.bashrc
```
e, no seu `.npmrc`, em vez de `<o-seu-token>` escreva `${GLAB_TOKEN}`

Depois:

```bash
npm install @ltplabs/aya-react
```

Obtenha um token no GitLab → Utilizador → Preferências → Tokens de Acesso (scope: `read_api`).

## Variáveis de ambiente

Crie um ficheiro `.env` na raiz do projecto (copie a partir de `.env.example`):

```ini
VITE_KEYCLOAK_URL=https://auth.example.com/
VITE_REALM=my-realm
VITE_OIDC_CLIENT_ID=my-client
```
